page: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by an L3 cache of 48 MB, L2 cache of 1 MB, and L1d cache of 48 KB. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It contains no local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on external premium storage options.

Benchmark data positions the D32ls_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with some notable strengths. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1682.0 and a multi-core score of 14191.0. Its image processing performance is strong, scoring 13651.0 on Geekbench, and it registers a strong Passmark memory score of 2756.62. Memory bandwidth tests show 150.05 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 69.83 GB/sec for large-block write operations. For lightweight language model tasks, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 293.87 tokens per second on a small 135M model, though performance drops significantly to a poor 2.28 tokens per second on a large 70B model due to memory and hardware constraints.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ls_v5 offers a dense allocation of compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations. However, the lack of local storage and GPUs, combined with the low 2.0 GB of RAM per core, represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive databases or heavy machine learning training. This server is best suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 64.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote premium storage. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for general compute, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 14191.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 36834.55. It exhibits strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation, but struggles significantly with larger 70B models. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-heavy tasks with low memory demands, making it well-suited for software compilation, web serving, and image processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v5 is an Intel-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. It delivers solid compute performance for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v5 is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, ideal for CPU-bound workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and a lean memory profile for efficient parallel processing.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Configured with 64 GB of RAM, offering a lean 2.0 GB per core memory ratio.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring external premium storage.
Strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation.
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight compute tasks.
